user_query:
I exported my Bitaxe Gamma miner's status snapshots and need a concise health report and machine-readable summary.

Read input/snapshots.jsonl (each line is a JSON object from /api/system/info with two added fields: "timestamp" as an ISO-8601 string and "ip"). Compute metrics and write the following files:

1) output/summary.json — JSON object with these keys (exact names):
- miner_ip: string (choose the most frequent "ip" in the snapshots; if tied, pick the lexicographically smallest)
- snapshot_count: integer (number of valid lines parsed)
- first_timestamp: ISO-8601 string (earliest)
- last_timestamp: ISO-8601 string (latest)
- shares_accepted: integer (sum of sharesAccepted across snapshots; treat missing/invalid as 0)
- shares_rejected: integer (sum of sharesRejected across snapshots; treat missing/invalid as 0)
- reject_rate: number rounded to 4 decimals (shares_rejected / (shares_accepted + shares_rejected); if denominator = 0, set to 0.0)
- avg_hashrate: number rounded to 2 decimals (mean of hashRate; ignore null/NaN/missing)
- avg_hashrate_1m: number rounded to 2 decimals (mean of hashRate_1m; ignore null/NaN/missing)
- avg_hashrate_10m: number rounded to 2 decimals (mean of hashRate_10m; ignore null/NaN/missing)
- avg_power: number rounded to 2 decimals (mean of power; ignore null/NaN/missing)
- avg_temp: number rounded to 2 decimals (mean of temp ignoring values of -1, null, NaN)
- max_temp: number rounded to 2 decimals (max of temp ignoring -1, null, NaN)
- min_temp: number rounded to 2 decimals (min of temp ignoring -1, null, NaN)
- best_bestDiff: number (maximum bestDiff across snapshots; if all missing, use 0)
- median_wifiRSSI: number rounded to 1 decimal (median of wifiRSSI; ignore null/NaN/missing)
- efficiency_gh_per_w: number rounded to 3 decimals (avg_hashrate / avg_power; if avg_power is 0 or missing, set to 0.0)

2) output/report.txt — a human-readable report including at least these labeled lines (one per line):
- "Miner IP: <value>"
- "Snapshot count: <value>"
- "Time range: <first_timestamp> to <last_timestamp>"
- "Hashrate (GH/s): avg=<avg_hashrate>, 1m=<avg_hashrate_1m>, 10m=<avg_hashrate_10m>"
- "Power (W): avg=<avg_power>"
- "Temperature (°C): avg=<avg_temp>, min=<min_temp>, max=<max_temp>"
- "Shares: accepted=<shares_accepted>, rejected=<shares_rejected>, reject_rate=<reject_rate>"
- "WiFi RSSI (dBm): median=<median_wifiRSSI>"
- "Efficiency (GH/s per W): <efficiency_gh_per_w>"

3) output/alerts.txt — zero or more alerts, one per line, using ONLY the following codes (exact capitalization, no extra text):
- OVERHEAT (trigger if any temp > 85°C)
- FAN_FAILURE_SUSPECTED (trigger if any snapshot has fanspeed < 20 while temp > 70)
- POOL_DISCONNECTED (trigger if any snapshot has missing/empty stratumURL or stratumPort is 0 or missing)
- HIGH_REJECT_RATE (trigger if reject_rate > 0.05)
- LOW_HASHRATE_10M (trigger if avg_hashrate_10m < 200)
- WEAK_WIFI (trigger if median_wifiRSSI < -70)

Notes and rules:
- Treat any missing or non-numeric values as absent; exclude them from means/medians and from min/max if not valid numbers. For shares sums, missing/non-numeric counts as 0.
- Ignore snapshots that are not valid JSON. For valid JSON lines, if timestamp is missing or malformed, skip that snapshot completely.
- Rounding must follow the rules above. Do not include extra keys in output/summary.json.
- Make sure all three files are created under output/.
